name: Les
description_short: LES Federal Credit Union is a community-focused financial institution serving Baton Rouge, LA, offering comprehensive banking services including checking, savings, loans, and digital banking solutions.
description_long: LES Federal Credit Union (LES FCU) is a federally chartered credit union serving the Baton Rouge, Louisiana community and surrounding service areas. Based on NCUA data, the institution manages thousands of member accounts across multiple product lines and maintains a physical branch presence with accessible member services. The organization positions itself as a community-focused alternative to traditional banks, emphasizing member benefits and personalized financial solutions. LES FCU offers a comprehensive suite of retail banking products including checking accounts (Share Draft Accounts with 2,148 active accounts), savings accounts (5,502 active regular share savings accounts), and certificates of deposit (153 active CDs). The credit union also provides tax-advantaged retirement accounts (IRA/KEOGH with 45 active accounts), unsecured credit cards to 900 members totaling $1.67 million in outstanding balances, and flexible loan products including auto loans and mortgages. Digital banking capabilities include mobile banking, online bill pay, account aggregation, e-statements, and online loan payments. LES FCU distinguishes itself through its credit union structure, which typically offers lower fees and competitive rates compared to traditional banks. The institution emphasizes member benefits and exclusive discounts, maintains physical branch locations for in-person service, and provides financial planning and wealth management services. The credit union model allows members to become partial owners and benefit from profit-sharing through competitive rates and reduced fees. As a regional credit union with approximately 2,148 checking accounts and 5,502 savings accounts, LES FCU serves a defined community rather than operating nationally. The organization's credit card portfolio (11.20% APR baseline) and loan offerings are moderate in scale. Member access is limited to those who meet membership eligibility requirements tied to the Baton Rouge area and associated service fields. Digital banking infrastructure appears modern with mobile and online capabilities, though availability of specialized services like investment management may be more limited than larger institutions.
services: Regular savings accounts with competitive interest rates
Share Certificates (CDs) with low minimum balances
IRA and KEOGH retirement planning accounts
pros: Free checking accounts with no monthly fees and free checks
5,502 active savings accounts offering competitive interest rates
Mobile banking and online banking platform for account management and bill pay
Credit union membership structure providing lower fees and better rates than traditional banks
Flexible auto loans and mortgage options tailored to personal needs
Tax-advantaged retirement accounts (IRA/KEOGH) for long-term savings
Account aggregation feature to view all financial accounts in one place
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to Baton Rouge, LA and defined service areas
Membership eligibility requirements restrict access compared to nationwide banks
Small credit card portfolio ($1.67 million) with limited rewards program details mentioned
Only 153 active CDs and 45 IRA accounts suggest limited adoption of certain savings products
No information provided about investment or brokerage services despite claims of 'expert investment planning'
best_for: Baton Rouge-area residents seeking community-focused banking with lower fees
Members wanting comprehensive financial services from checking to retirement planning
Consumers prioritizing digital convenience with mobile and online banking
Long-term savers interested in CDs and tax-advantaged retirement accounts
diagnosis: LES FCU is best for Baton Rouge-area residents seeking full-service community banking with the member benefits and lower fees typical of credit unions. The primary caveat is geographic restriction—membership and branch access are limited to the Baton Rouge area and defined service fields, making this institution unavailable to most consumers outside Louisiana.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
